name: From Lake Matemale to Creu Pass then to Llose Pass
name_original: Du lac de Matemale au col de la Creu puis de la Llose
region_intro: Hiking around Matemale offers diverse landscapes within the Natural Park of the Catalan Pyrenees, centered around the artificial Lac de Matemale at 1500 meters altitude. The region is characterized by pine forests, the majestic peaks of the Pyrenees, and the high plateau of Capcir, an ancient glacial basin. Trails traverse areas between the granite Carlit Massif and Madrès, providing access to a rich natural heritage. Best hiking trails around Matemale The most popular hiking route is Lake Matemale – Lake Matemale loop from Matemale, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers continuous views of the lake and its surrounding forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Along the Têt river – Plà de Barrès River and Forest loop from Jaça d'en Calvet, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail follows the Têt river through varied forest terrain. Local hikers also love the Col de la Creu – Refuge du Coll de Torn loop from Col de Creu, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through mountain passes and towards a refuge, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Matemale is defined by its prominent lake, surrounding pine forests, and views of the Pyrenees peaks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy lakeside walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Matemale are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Matemale's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Matemale?
A: Matemale offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 40 trails. These range from easy lakeside strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes near Lac de Matemale?
A: Yes, the area around Lac de Matemale is ideal for families. You can enjoy accessible paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3994360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Matemale – Lake Matemale loop from Matemale&lt;/a&gt;, which offers continuous views of the lake and surrounding forests. The lake itself provides supervised swimming and other activities, making it a great family destination.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Matemale?
A: Matemale is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter pine forests, the majestic peaks of the Pyrenees, and the artificial Lac de Matemale. Higher altitude hikes can lead to stunning spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4554858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Llaret&lt;/a&gt; or offer panoramic views from passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2158563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Tossa&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the Capcir, a high plateau with remarkable natural heritage.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced adventurers in the Matemale area?
A: While many trails are moderate, more challenging mountain hikes are available for experienced adventurers. Some routes lead to peaks like Mount Llaret or Pérics. For a more adventurous terrain, consider exploring the dizzying passages of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/260391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Matemale?
A: Matemale offers a refreshing climate, mild and sunny, influenced by the Mediterranean. Summer is popular, with lake temperatures ideal for swimming (19-23°C). The area is also beautiful in spring and autumn, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Winter provides opportunities for snow activities in nearby resorts, though some higher hiking trails may be inaccessible.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Matemale?
A: Yes, many trails in Matemale are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20536107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Creu – Refuge du Coll de Torn loop from Col de Creu&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through mountain passes. Another great loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6984148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matemale – Tower of the Creu loop from Matemale&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Matemale?
A: The routes in Matemale are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the continuous views of Lac de Matemale, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Can I find hikes that follow rivers or through forests in Matemale?
A: Absolutely. The region features varied terrain, including beautiful forest paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1233246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along the Têt river – Plà de Barrès River and Forest loop from Jaça d'en Calvet&lt;/a&gt; follows the Têt river through varied forest terrain, offering a different perspective of the area's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots accessible by hiking in Matemale?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/612655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Coll del Pam (2005 m)&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas. Additionally, the Col de la Perche, reaching 1,579 meters, offers splendid 360-degree panoramic views of the surrounding mountains, valleys, and plains within the Catalan Pyrenees natural park.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Matemale?
A: Many trails in the Matemale area are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and check specific trail regulations, especially within protected natural areas like the Natura 2000 sites.

Q: Are there any hikes that lead to other lakes besides Lac de Matemale?
A: Yes, beyond Lac de Matemale, you can hike to other beautiful lakes. The higher altitude &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3578005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany de la Pradella&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example. Further afield, the stunning Lake Bouillouses, located at 2,016 meters, is surrounded by a network of smaller ponds, including the Carlit ponds, offering a more extensive lake-hopping experience.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in Matemale?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the trail's difficulty. Easy lakeside walks, like the Matemale loop, have minimal elevation changes (around 40 meters). Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39999912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Creu – Puig dels Agrellons loop from Col de Creu&lt;/a&gt;, can involve several hundred meters of ascent (around 310 meters), while more challenging mountain ascents will naturally feature greater elevation gains.
tips:
The coll de la Perche is a mountain pass that culminates at 1579 meters, on the plateau of La Perxa, in Alta Cerdanya, in Northern Catalonia. The port from the town of Olette has a lake length of more than 22 km, with an average of 4.2%.
From this point you have a fantastic view with the Canigó in the background.
Incredible coffee and stop to enjoy nature
